extraction.one_line_summary: VACEK Mathias won from breakaway at age 19 after Gazprom positioned three riders and pack miscalculated final 11km chase timing
tactical_patterns: Gazprom committed three riders to every breakaway attempt, creating numerical superiority when it finally succeeded
Peloton completely misjudged chase timing, staying at 1:22 with 11km remaining despite clear breakaway coordination
STRAKHOV in black jersey worked for teammates despite personal classification interest, revealing Gazprom team-first discipline
PHILIPSEN led bunch sprint behind breakaway showing teams prepared wrong finish scenario despite 180km flat profile

rider_signals.VACEK Mathias: positive: won stage aged 19 outsprinting experienced breakaway companions, shows precocious tactical maturity
rider_signals.STRAKHOV Dmitry: neutral: sacrificed stage win while holding classification jersey to deliver KOCHETKOV and VACEK
rider_signals.KOCHETKOV Pavel: neutral: Gazprom's designated finisher but beaten by younger VACEK in final meters
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: negative: led peloton sprint despite obvious breakaway success, suggests poor race reading or team miscommunication
rider_signals.TONELLI Alessandro: neutral: lone Bardiani rider after CAÑAVERAL dropped back, held on for fourth without team support
